$DAT_COMMON_MENU.Content Language: Sets how the DAT content is interpreted.
$DAT_COMMON_MENU.From Parameter: Uses the language set in parameters.

$VIEW_MENU.View: Controls how the DAT is displayed.

$AUDIO_DEVICES_MENU.Callbacks DAT: DAT used for audio device callbacks.

$CHOP_EXECUTE_MENU.CHOP Execute: Executes scripts based on CHOP changes.
$CHOP_EXECUTE_MENU.From Operator: Uses a specific operator as source.

$FILE_MENU.Write to TOE Save: Writes file when project is saved.

operators.what_it_is: Handles Art-Net related data, callbacks, and device configuration.
operators.mental_model: A control desk that lists lighting network devices and reacts to their signals.
operators.example: Monitor Art-Net devices and trigger scripts when lighting data changes.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It watches lighting network messages and tells TouchDesigner when something changes.

operators.what_it_is: Reads and reacts to Art-Net lighting network information.
operators.mental_model: A clipboard that lists lighting devices and listens for what they say.
operators.example: See which lighting controllers are connected and run a script when they send data.

Artnet.Columns: How the list is laid out.
Artnet.Pull Devices: Ask the network who is there.
Artnet.Local Addresses: Which network addresses this computer uses.
Artnet.Local Port: Which door the messages come in through.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It makes a list of lighting boxes and tells you when they talk.

$DAT_COMMON_MENU.Content Language: What kind of writing this is.
$DAT_COMMON_MENU.From Input: Figure it out from what comes in.

$VIEW_MENU.View: How you look at the DAT.
$VIEW_MENU.Edit: Open it so you can type.
$VIEW_MENU.Word Wrap: Make long lines stay on the screen.

$CHOP_EXECUTE_MENU.While Off on Frequency: React every so often while off.

$FILE_MENU.File: How this connects to a file.
$FILE_MENU.Sync to File: Keep file and DAT the same.
$FILE_MENU.Load on Start: Open the file when the project opens.
$FILE_MENU.Write to TOE Save: Save the file with the project.

operators.what_it_is: Shows information from Art-Net lighting networks.
operators.mental_model: A list that listens to lighting gear.
operators.example: See which lighting controllers are connected and react to them.

Artnet.Pull Devices: Ask the network who is there.
Artnet.Local Addresses: Which network paths this computer uses.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It makes a list of lighting boxes and listens when they talk.

operators.what_it_is: Turns CHOP channels into text or tables.
operators.mental_model: A translator that turns moving numbers into readable lists.
operators.example: Turn animation values into a table you can look up or save.

CHOP to.Output: Choose what kind of data comes out.

Convert.How to Text: Rules for turning numbers into words.
Convert.To Table: Rules for turning numbers into rows and columns.
Convert.Split Cells At: Break text into cells at a character.

DAT Execute.Size Change: React when rows or columns change.
DAT Execute.Start of Frame: Run at the beginning of a frame.
DAT Execute.End of Frame: Run at the end of a frame.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It turns moving numbers into words or tables you can read.

operators.what_it_is: Shows errors and warnings from operators.
operators.mental_model: A problem list that keeps track of what went wrong.
operators.example: See which operator is failing and why.

Error.Active: Turn error tracking on or off.
Error.Type: What kind of problem it is.
Error.Log Current Errors: Keep a record of current problems.

Callbacks.Stat Current Node or Specified Operator: Watch this operator or another one.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It makes a list of problems so you can see what broke.

operators.what_it_is: Lists and manages Ether Dream laser devices.
operators.mental_model: A device list for laser hardware.
operators.example: Find laser controllers on the network.

Etherdream.Pull Devices: Ask the network who is there.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It finds laser boxes on the network and lists them.

operators.what_it_is: Runs expressions using table data and writes the results.
operators.mental_model: A calculator that reads tables.
operators.example: Use table values to compute new numbers.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It does math using tables and writes down the answers.

operators.what_it_is: Looks inside an operator and lists what it contains.
operators.mental_model: Opening a box to see everything inside it.
operators.example: See what variables, settings, and values an operator is holding.

Examine.Source Operator Storage: Read stored values from the operator.
Examine.Type: What kind of value it is.
Examine.Max Levels: How deep it is allowed to go.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It peeks inside something and makes a list of what’s in there.

operators.what_it_is: Runs code when specific events happen.
operators.mental_model: A doorbell that runs code when something occurs.
operators.example: Run a script when the project starts or stops.

Execute.Create: React when an operator is made.
Execute.Frame Start: React at the start of a frame.
Execute.Frame End: React at the end of a frame.
Execute.Play State Changed: React when play stops or starts.

File.File: How this connects to a file.
File.Sync to File: Keep file and DAT the same.
File.Load on Start: Open the file when the project opens.
File.Write on TOE Save: Save the file with the project.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It runs instructions when something happens.

operators.what_it_is: Keeps a running list of messages in the order they arrive.
operators.mental_model: A line where the first thing in is the first thing out.
operators.example: Collect log messages and keep only the most recent ones.

FIFO.Current Node or Specified Operator: Watch this operator or another one.
FIFO.Keep Row First: Keep the oldest line at the top.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It’s a list that keeps messages in the order they came in.

operators.what_it_is: Reads a text file into a DAT.
operators.mental_model: Opening a notebook and copying the words inside.
operators.example: Load a text file or CSV so TouchDesigner can read it.

Convert.Text to Table: Turn text into rows and columns.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It reads words from a file and brings them into TouchDesigner.

operators.what_it_is: Writes the DAT’s contents to a file.
operators.mental_model: Saving what you wrote onto paper.
operators.example: Save a table or text to a file on disk.

File Out.Append Only: Only add new text to the end.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It puts the words from TouchDesigner into a file.

operators.what_it_is: Lists files and folders from the computer.
operators.mental_model: Looking inside a folder and writing down what’s there.
operators.example: Make a table of all images inside a directory.

Folder.Active: Turn folder watching on or off.
Folder.File Type: What kinds of files to list.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It makes a list of files in a folder.

operators.what_it_is: Shows information about an operator.
operators.mental_model: A fact card about something.
operators.example: See stats and details about an operator.

Info.Info Type: What kind of info to show.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It tells you facts about another operator.

operators.what_it_is: Adds rows or columns into a table.
operators.mental_model: Sliding new boxes into a grid.
operators.example: Add a new row to a table when something happens.

Insert.Index: Put it at a specific spot.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It puts new rows or columns into a table.

operators.what_it_is: Reads, filters, and reshapes JSON data.
operators.mental_model: Picking pieces out of a big labeled box.
operators.example: Pull specific values from a JSON file into a table.

JSON.Output: What kind of result comes out.
JSON.Table: Put results into rows and columns.
JSON.Hold Last: Keep last result if nothing new.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It pulls out the parts you want from a big data note.

operators.what_it_is: Reads keyboard presses as data.
operators.mental_model: A listener that writes down which keys you press.
operators.example: Trigger actions when certain keys are pressed.

Keyboard In.Active: Turn key listening on or off.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It listens to the keyboard and writes down which keys you press.

operators.what_it_is: Finds values in a table using another value as a key.
operators.mental_model: A chart where you point at one thing and get the matching thing.
operators.example: Use an ID to find a name or setting in a table.
operators.explain_like_im_five: You give it a clue, and it looks up the answer in a chart.

operators.what_it_is: Reads information about media files.
operators.mental_model: A label that tells you facts about a video or sound file.
operators.example: Get duration, size, or format from a movie file.

Media File In.File Open Timeout: How long to wait for the file.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It tells you facts about a movie or sound file.

operators.what_it_is: Combines multiple DATs into one.
operators.mental_model: Putting several tables together into one big table.
operators.example: Join data from different sources into a single table.

Merge.Concatenate With: Join text together with a character.
Merge.Append Unmatched: Keep things that don’t line up.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It sticks tables together in different ways.

operators.what_it_is: Shows incoming MIDI messages as data.
operators.mental_model: A note pad that writes down music button presses.
operators.example: See which MIDI note or control was sent.

MIDI Event.Connect Active: Turn MIDI listening on or off.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It listens to music gear and writes down what it says.

operators.what_it_is: Lists connected MIDI devices and their messages.
operators.mental_model: A sign-in sheet for music gear.
operators.example: See which MIDI keyboard is connected and what it sends.

MIDI In.Active: Turn MIDI listening on or off.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It shows which music controllers are plugged in.

operators.what_it_is: Lists connected displays and their properties.
operators.mental_model: A map of all the screens attached to the computer.
operators.example: Find screen sizes and positions for multiple monitors.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It tells you about all the screens plugged in.

operators.what_it_is: Reads MPCDI projector calibration data.
operators.mental_model: A blueprint that tells projectors where to aim.
operators.example: Load projector mapping for a multi-projector setup.

MPCDI.Output by Single Region or Full Buffer: Choose part or all of the data.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It tells projectors how to line up their pictures.

operators.what_it_is: Connects to an MQTT server and exchanges messages.
operators.mental_model: A mailbox that talks to the internet.
operators.example: Receive sensor data from another computer.

MQTT Client.Active: Turn the connection on or off.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It sends and receives messages over the internet.

operators.what_it_is: Reads touch and gesture input from touchscreens or trackpads.
operators.mental_model: A listener that writes down where fingers touch the screen.
operators.example: Track finger positions for a touch-based interface.

Multi-Touch In.Active: Turn touch listening on or off.
Multi-Touch In.Output: What kind of data is written.
Multi-Touch In.Raw Events: Every touch event as it happens.
Multi-Touch In.Relative Position: Touch position relative to a panel.
Multi-Touch In.By Hierarchy: Use panel order to block touches.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It watches fingers touching the screen and writes down where they land.

operators.what_it_is: Finds and tracks NDI video sources on the network.
operators.mental_model: A scanner that looks for shared video feeds.
operators.example: See which computers are sharing video over NDI.

NDI.Monitors: Which screens or sources to watch.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It looks around the network to find video streams.

operators.what_it_is: Passes data through unchanged.
operators.mental_model: A straight pipe.
operators.example: Use it to organize or name a data stream.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It does nothing on purpose.

operators.what_it_is: Runs code when operators change or do things.
operators.mental_model: A watcher that reacts when something happens.
operators.example: Run code when an operator is created or deleted.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It runs instructions when operators change.

operators.what_it_is: Searches for operators that match rules.
operators.mental_model: A search tool for your network.
operators.example: Find all TOPs with a certain name or tag.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It helps you find things in a big project.

operators.what_it_is: Sends and receives network messages using CSN.
operators.mental_model: A walkie-talkie that can talk one-to-one or one-to-many.
operators.example: Send control messages to other machines on the network.

CSN.Messaging: Send messages one at a time.
CSN.Local Address: Which network address this machine uses.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It sends notes to other computers and listens for replies.

operators.what_it_is: Passes data out of a component.
operators.mental_model: A door that lets data leave the room.
operators.example: Send a table from inside a component to the outside.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It lets data go out to the rest of the project.

operators.what_it_is: Runs code when panel values change.
operators.mental_model: A button watcher that reacts when you touch things.
operators.example: Run code when a button is pressed or released.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It runs instructions when you touch buttons.

$OSC_CONNECT_MENU.Messaging: Send messages one at a time.
$OSC_CONNECT_MENU.Local Address: Which network address this machine uses.

$RECEIVED_MESSAGES_MENU.Current Node or Specified Operator: Watch this operator or another one.

operators.what_it_is: Receives OSC messages from the network and writes them into a DAT.
operators.mental_model: A mailbox for network messages.
operators.example: Receive OSC control messages from another app and turn them into a table.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It listens for messages coming in from another computer and writes them down.

operators.what_it_is: Runs code when individual parameters change.
operators.mental_model: A watcher that reacts when a knob or slider is touched.
operators.example: Run code when a parameter value changes or gets pulsed.

Parameter Execute.On Pulse: React when a button is pressed.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It runs instructions when a setting changes.

operators.what_it_is: Runs code when groups of parameters change.
operators.mental_model: A watcher that listens to many knobs at once.
operators.example: React when any parameter in a group changes.

Par Group Execute.On Pulse: React when a button is pressed.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It reacts when a whole group of settings changes.

operators.what_it_is: Shows timing and performance problems while the project runs.
operators.mental_model: A stopwatch that points at what is slowing things down.
operators.example: See why frames are dropping or taking too long.

Perform.Active: Turn performance tracking on or off.
Perform.When Frame Length Exceeds Threshold: Trigger when frames take too long.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It tells you what part is making things slow.

operators.what_it_is: Turns POP particle data into a table.
operators.mental_model: A translator that turns particle info into rows and columns.
operators.example: Take particle positions and colors and put them into a DAT table.

POP to.Download Type: What kind of POP data to pull.
POP to.Thin Out Range: Only take part of the data.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It turns a bunch of particle facts into a table you can read.

operators.what_it_is: Figures out what you are pointing at in a render and reports the hit results.
operators.mental_model: Touching the screen and asking, ‘what did I touch?’
operators.example: Click a 3D object and get its position and ID back as data.

Render Pick.Pick Radius: How big the pick area is.
Render Pick.Custom Pick Camera: Use a specific camera for picking.
Render Pick.Include Non-Pickable Objects: Also pick things not marked pickable.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It tells you what you clicked on and where you clicked it.

operators.what_it_is: Changes the order of rows or columns in a table.
operators.mental_model: Rearranging cards into a new order.
operators.example: Move important columns to the front of a table.

Reorder.Reorder Columns or Rows: Choose whether rows or columns move.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It changes the order of things in a table.

operators.what_it_is: Runs custom Python code stored in a DAT.
operators.mental_model: A note with instructions that can be run.
operators.example: Write custom logic that runs inside the network.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It runs your own instructions.

operators.what_it_is: Picks specific rows or columns from a table.
operators.mental_model: Highlighting only the parts you want.
operators.example: Keep only certain rows and columns from a table.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It keeps only the rows and columns you want.

operators.what_it_is: Reads data coming from a serial port.
operators.mental_model: Listening to a cable and writing down what it says.
operators.example: Receive sensor data from an Arduino.

Serial.Connect Active: Turn serial listening on or off.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It listens to a wire and writes down the messages.

operators.what_it_is: Lists available serial devices on the system.
operators.mental_model: A list of plugs you can use.
operators.example: Find which serial ports are available.

Serial Device.Usage: What the device is used for.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It shows which serial plugs are available.

operators.what_it_is: Sends and receives real-time messages using Socket.IO.
operators.mental_model: A live chat line between programs.
operators.example: Receive live updates from a web app.

Socket IO.Active: Turn the connection on or off.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It talks back and forth with another app in real time.

operators.what_it_is: Turns geometry data into a table.
operators.mental_model: Writing down facts about shapes.
operators.example: Put point positions from a mesh into a DAT.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It turns shape information into a list.

operators.what_it_is: Reorders rows or columns based on rules.
operators.mental_model: Putting things in order.
operators.example: Sort rows alphabetically or by number.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It puts rows or columns in the order you want.

operators.what_it_is: Finds and replaces text in a table.
operators.mental_model: Crossing out words and writing new ones.
operators.example: Replace old names with new ones.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It swaps words in a table.

operators.what_it_is: Chooses one DAT from many.
operators.mental_model: A switch that picks one path.
operators.example: Choose which table is active.

Switch.Extend: What happens if the index is out of range.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It picks which table to use.

operators.what_it_is: Creates and edits a table of rows and columns.
operators.mental_model: A spreadsheet you can type into.
operators.example: Manually enter values or load a table from a file.

Table.Load on Start: Load the file when the project opens.
Table.Write on TOE Save: Save the table with the project.

Fill.Fill by Column: Fill one column at a time.
Fill.Fill by Row: Fill one row at a time.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It’s a grid where you write things in boxes.

operators.what_it_is: Sends and receives data over a TCP network connection.
operators.mental_model: A phone line between two computers.
operators.example: Receive messages from another app on the same network.

TCP/IP.Active: Turn the connection on or off.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It talks to another computer using a cable made of numbers.

operators.what_it_is: Holds and edits plain text.
operators.mental_model: A text note inside TouchDesigner.
operators.example: Write notes, code, or templates.

Text.Sync to File: Keep text and file the same.
Text.Load on Start: Load the file when the project opens.
Text.Write on TOE Save: Save the text with the project.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It’s a place to write words.

operators.what_it_is: Receives touch data from another device over the network.
operators.mental_model: Someone touching a screen far away and you feeling it here.
operators.example: Receive touch input from a phone or tablet.

Touch In.Active: Turn touch receiving on or off.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It listens for touches coming from another device.

operators.what_it_is: Sends touch data to another device or program.
operators.mental_model: Sending finger touches out through a wire.
operators.example: Send touch positions to another TouchDesigner project.

Touch Out.Local Port Mode: How the local port is chosen.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It sends your touch information to somewhere else.

operators.what_it_is: Swaps rows and columns in a table.
operators.mental_model: Turning a grid sideways.
operators.example: Flip rows into columns.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It turns rows into columns.

operators.what_it_is: Receives TUIO touch and object data.
operators.mental_model: Listening for touch objects sent over the network.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It listens for touch shapes and movements sent from somewhere else.

operators.what_it_is: Receives messages sent using UDP.
operators.mental_model: Catching notes thrown quickly across the room.
operators.example: Receive fast control messages from another app.

operators.what_it_is: Lists video input and output devices on the computer.
operators.mental_model: A list of cameras and video gear you can plug in.
operators.example: See which webcams or capture cards are available.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It shows all the cameras and video plugs your computer can see.

operators.what_it_is: Sends web requests and receives responses.
operators.mental_model: Asking a website a question and waiting for the answer.
operators.example: Request data from an online API.

Web Client.Active: Turn web requests on or off.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It asks websites for information and writes down the reply.

operators.what_it_is: Lets TouchDesigner act like a website server.
operators.mental_model: Opening a tiny website inside your project.
operators.example: Serve web pages or data to other devices.

Web Server.Active: Turn the server on or off.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It lets other computers visit your project like a website.

operators.what_it_is: Sends and receives live audio/video and data with very low delay.
operators.mental_model: A live video call line.
operators.example: Stream camera video to another computer in real time.

WebRTC.Connection Active: Turn the connection on or off.
WebRTC.Callback Stat: Run reactions when connection state changes.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It lets two computers talk face-to-face very fast.

operators.what_it_is: Keeps a live two-way connection to another program.
operators.mental_model: An always-open phone call.
operators.example: Send and receive live messages from a web app.

WebSocket.Connect Active: Turn the connection on or off.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It keeps a live chat open with another app.

operators.what_it_is: Reads and reshapes XML or HTML data.
operators.mental_model: Taking a tagged document and turning it into a table.
operators.example: Pull values out of an XML file into rows and columns.

XML.Label Type: What kind of label it is.
